attributes.action: Close legal gap allowing adults to have sex with 16–17-year-olds
attributes.additional_details: Although the age of consent is 16, current laws do not adequately protect young people from exploitation by significantly older adults. This gap can leave 16–17-year-olds vulnerable to grooming, coercion, and abuse. Close the gap by ensuring that it is a criminal offence if an adult aged 20 or over engages in sexual activity with a 16–17-year-old where there is a substantial age difference (for example, more than 4 years).
attributes.background: Introduce legislation to close legal loopholes that allow adults to engage in sexual relationships with 16–17-year-olds. Close the  legal gap allowing adults to have sex with 16–17-year-olds. 30 year olds should not legally be able to have sexual relationships with under 18s.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign the following petition, which calls for similar action:

Raise the age of consent to 18 with peer exception for 16-17 year olds
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/767458
